                                                                                 CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                                            In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                                            Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown be ow:

                                                                                          Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   July   Aug.   Sept.   Oct.    Nov.
                                       Household data
                                    Annual averages                                        x

                                    Revised seasonally adjusted series and
                                     current seasonal factors                                     X

                                     Seasonally adjusted data
                                     Persons not in labor force                            X                    X            X                        X

                                       Establishment data

                                      Industry divisions (preliminary)                     X
                                      Industry detail (final)                                           X
                                     Women employment (National)                                  X                X             X                 X
                                     National data adjusted to new benchmarks                                                         (1)
                                     Revised seasonally adjusted series and
                                      current seasonal factors                                                                        (1)
                                     State and area annual averages                                                X
                                     Area definitions                                                              X

                                           The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The September 1971 issue
                                    marks the introduction of March 1970 benchmarks.

                                                                  Technical Note
                    The statistics in this periodical are compiled from three                  Employment
                major sources: (1) Household interviews, (2) reports from em-
                ployers, and (3) administrative statistics of unemployment                       Coverage. The household survey definition of employment
                insurance systems.                                                          comprises wage and salary workers (including domestics and
                    Data based on household interviews are obtained from a                  other private household workers), self-employed persons, and
                sample survey of the population 16 years of age and over. The               unpaid workers who worked 15 hours or more during the survey
                survey is conducted each month by the Bureau of the Census                  week in family-operated enterprises. Employment in both
                for the Bureau of Labor Statistics and provides comprehensive               agricultural and nonagricultural industries is included. The pay-
                data on the labor force, the employed and the unemployed,                   roll survey covers only wage and salary employees on the pay-
                including such characteristics as age, sex, color, marital status,          rolls of nonagricultural establishments.
